channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Key constituencies in Kerala's 2026 elections: A close look at Nemom, Pala, and others
item.description: The 2026 Kerala Legislative Assembly elections are shaping up to be crucial as several constituencies emerge as high-stakes battlegrounds, reflecting changing political dynamics in the state. While th...

item.title: Al-Aqsa Mosque Reopens: Palestinians Gather in Joy After Extended Closure
item.description: After a 40-day period of closure imposed by Israeli authorities, Al-Aqsa Mosque in occupied East Jerusalem joyfully reopened its gates at dawn on Thursday, April 9, 2026. The reopening, coinciding wit...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Attack on Senator Godfrey Osotsi in Kisumu
item.description: Police in Kisumu have opened investigations following a violent attack on Vihiga Senator Godfrey Osotsi at a local restaurant on Wednesday afternoon. The senator was assaulted by a group of hooded ind...

item.title: Oil Pipeline Attack in Saudi Arabia Raises Concerns Amidst Ceasefire
item.description: Following a ceasefire agreement, a critical East-West oil pipeline in Saudi Arabia has been attacked, escalating tensions and disrupting energy supply chain concerns. The incident underscores the ongo...

item.title: Government meeting on fuel imports amid Middle East crisis triggers policy changes
item.description: A crisis meeting chaired by Chief of Staff Felix Koskei on March 9, 2026, under the National Security Council Committee (NSCC), instructed the State Department of Petroleum to explore alternative fuel...

item.title: A History of Papal Visits to Africa and Controversies They Stirred
item.description: Pope Leo XIV's upcoming visit to four African countries follows a long history of papal visits to the continent, where over 20% of the world’s Catholics live. From Pope Paul VI's groundbreaking 1969 t...

item.title: Multiple Tribal Leaders Join Congress from BJP in Dahod, Gujarat
item.description: In a significant political development in Gujarat's Dahod district, multiple tribal leaders have switched allegiance from the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) to the Indian National Congress. This move co...

item.title: Artemis II Mission: Historic Crew Surveys Moon's Surface
item.description: The Artemis II mission, a significant milestone in space exploration, recently concluded with a crew of four astronauts – including the first woman, first Black astronaut, and first Canadian – success...

item.title: Government introduces new law to let disabled claimants work or volunteer without losing benefits
item.description: The UK government has introduced new legislation aimed at allowing sick and disabled people to attempt returning to work without the fear of losing their welfare support. The Department for Work and P...

item.title: Ibadan Deploys 5,000 Community Guards to Bolster Security
item.description: The Olubadan of Ibadanland, Oba Rashidi Ladoja, don officially launch 5,000 community security personnel, dem wey dem call Ibadan Guards. This na serious move to address the wahala of insecurity wey d...

item.title: New Study Challenges Conventional Sprinting Techniques, Emphasizing Individual Traits for Speed
item.description: A new study led by Flinders University and published in Sports Medicine challenges the traditional belief that there is a single, ideal sprinting technique for elite athletes. The research reveals tha...

item.title: Former University of Manitoba Law Dean Wanted Across Canada on Criminal Charges
item.description: Canadian police have issued a Canada-wide arrest warrant for a former dean of the University of Manitoba’s Faculty of Law. According to CTV News Winnipeg, authorities are searching for the man in conn...

item.title: Traffic Advisory Issued for Nairobi CBD During President Ruto's Official Events
item.description: The National Police Service (NPS) has issued a traffic advisory for Nairobi's Central Business District (CBD) ahead of President William Ruto's historic address at City Hall on Thursday, April 9, 2026...

item.title: Clarkson’s Farm producer reveals why the show will eventually end
item.description: Jeremy Clarkson's popular Prime Video series 'Clarkson’s Farm' is set to return for its fifth season, but its producer, Andy Willman, has revealed that the show has a finite lifespan. Willman explaine...

item.title: Corporate Retreat in Honduras Faces Serious Logistical and Health Issues
item.description: In 2017, Plex organized a $500,000 'Survivor'-style corporate retreat in Honduras for its employees, which quickly encountered numerous difficulties. The CEO, Keith Valory, fell seriously ill with E. ...

item.title: Peaceful short breaks near Johannesburg for nature lovers
item.description: For Joburg-based travellers wanting a quick escape without trekking to the Drakensberg, there are several scenic destinations nearby that offer tranquillity, fresh air, and plenty of charm. The Dinoke...

item.title: Iran Provides Alternative Shipping Routes for Strait of Hormuz During Temporary Ceasefire
item.description: Iran don announce say dem don open alternative routes for ships wey dey travel through the Strait of Hormuz because dem dey fear sea mines for the main channel. This matter come after Iran and US agre...

item.title: Fragile US-Iran Ceasefire Strained as Regional Tensions Escalate
item.description: CNN's live coverage details the fragile state of the recently announced two-week ceasefire between the United States and Iran, which is under intense strain following multiple regional developments. I...

item.title: Australian Community Media Faces Financial Difficulties Amidst Antony Catalano's Personal Challenges
item.description: Troubles continue to mount for Antony Catalano, the co-owner of Australian Community Media (ACM) alongside billionaire Alex Waislitz. The media company, which publishes newspapers like the Canberra Ti...

item.title: Quebec’s Ongoing Abortion Access Issues: A Personal Account of Long Wait Times and Limited Services
item.description: In 2023, Rose Gervais from Gaspé, Quebec, was forced to travel 900 km to Montreal for a surgical abortion after being told her pregnancy was no longer viable. The procedure, which should have been ava...
